mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate signals weak labor market conditions that depress corporate earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment, or the correlation between UNRATE and SPX returns falls below -0.25.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 225 in CPIAUCSL triggers central bank tightening expectations, increasing discount rates and reducing equity valuations, which predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 225, or the predictive power of CPIAUCSL on SPX returns is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in FEDFUNDS and increases in CPIAUCSL signal stagflationary pressures, creating a negative earnings environment that predicts S&P 500 underperformance over 60 days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL fails to explain > 10% of SPX return variance in out-of-sample tests, or SPX returns show positive correlation with stagflation indicators.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) indicate strong demand for construction, boosting related sectors and predicting positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of instances where HOUST exceeds its 3-month moving average, or the correlation between HOUST and SPX returns is below 0.15.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production (INDPRO) reflects robust manufacturing activity, which drives corporate profits and predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 60% of instances where INDPRO exceeds its 6-month high, or the predictive power of INDPRO on SPX returns is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in real sales (RSAFS) indicates strong consumer demand, which drives corporate revenues and predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 60% of instances where RSAFS exceeds its 12-month high, or the correlation between RSAFS and SPX returns is below 0.20.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) signals a tightening labor market, increasing wage pressures and corporate costs, which predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where PAYEMS exceeds its 3-month high, or the correlation between PAYEMS and SPX returns is below -0.15.
